𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Fourteen — Part Three

The Book of Melakhim Aleph

MELAKHIM ALEPH

The First Book of Kings

Chapters 13 through 18

The Divided Kingdom — Akhav — Eliyahu the Prophet — Karmel — The Fire of YHWH

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

No complete DSS manuscript for Melakhim Aleph

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTERS 13 THROUGH 18 — THE PROPHETS AND AKHAV

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 13 The Man of Creator From Yehudah — The Withered Hand — The Old Prophet — The Lion Kills the Man of Creator

1 And behold, a man of Creator came out of Yehudah by the word of YHWH to Beit-El. Yarovam was standing by the altar to make offerings.

2 And the man cried against the altar by the word of YHWH and said, O altar, altar, thus says YHWH: Behold, a son shall be born to the house of Dawid, Yoshiyahu by name, and he shall sacrifice on you the priests of the high places who make offerings on you, and human bones shall be burned on you.

3 And he gave a sign the same day, saying, This is the sign that YHWH has spoken: Behold, the altar shall be torn down, and the ashes that are on it shall be poured out.

4 And when the king heard the word of the man of Creator, which he cried against the altar at Beit-El, Yarovam stretched out his hand from the altar, saying, Seize him. And his hand, which he stretched out against him, dried up, so that he could not draw it back to himself.

5 The altar also was torn down, and the ashes poured out from the altar, according to the sign that the man of Creator had given by the word of YHWH.

6 And the king said to the man of Creator, Entreat now the favor of YHWH your Creator, and pray for me, that my hand may be restored to me. And the man of Creator entreated YHWH, and the king's hand was restored to him and became as it was before.

7 And the king said to the man of Creator, Come home with me, and refresh yourself, and I will give you a reward.

8 And the man of Creator said to the king, If you give me half your house, I will not go in with you. And I will not eat bread or drink water in this place,

9 For so was it commanded me by the word of YHWH, saying, You shall neither eat bread nor drink water nor return by the way that you came.

10 So he went another way and did not return by the way that he came to Beit-El.

11 Now there lived an old prophet in Beit-El. And his sons came and told him all that the man of Creator had done that day in Beit-El. They also told to their father the words that he had spoken to the king.

12 And their father said to them, Which way did he go? And his sons showed him the way that the man of Creator who came from Yehudah had gone.

13 And he said to his sons, Saddle the donkey for me. So they saddled the donkey for him and he mounted it.

14 And he went after the man of Creator and found him sitting under a terebinth. And he said to him, Are you the man of Creator who came from Yehudah? And he said, I am.

15 Then he said to him, Come home with me and eat bread.

16 And he said, I may not return with you, or go in with you, neither will I eat bread nor drink water with you in this place,

17 For it was said to me by the word of YHWH, You shall neither eat bread nor drink water there, nor return by the way that you came.

18 And he said to him, I also am a prophet as you are, and an angel spoke to me by the word of YHWH, saying, Bring him back with you into your house that he may eat bread and drink water. But he lied to him.

19 So he went back with him and ate bread in his house and drank water.

20 And as they sat at the table, the word of YHWH came to the prophet who had brought him back.

21 And he cried to the man of Creator who came from Yehudah, Thus says YHWH, Because you have disobeyed the word of YHWH and have not kept the command that YHWH your Creator commanded you,

22 But have come back and have eaten bread and drunk water in the place of which he said to you, Eat no bread and drink no water, your body shall not come to the tomb of your fathers.

23 And after he had eaten bread and drunk, he saddled the donkey for the prophet whom he had brought back.

24 And as he went, a lion met him on the road and killed him. And his body was thrown in the road, and the donkey stood beside it — the lion also stood beside the body.

25 And behold, men passed by and saw the body thrown in the road and the lion standing beside the body. And they came and told it in the city where the old prophet lived.

26 And when the prophet who had brought him back from the way heard of it, he said, It is the man of Creator who disobeyed the word of YHWH. Therefore YHWH has given him to the lion, which has torn him and killed him, according to the word that YHWH spoke to him.

27 And he said to his sons, Saddle the donkey for me. And they saddled it.

28 And he went and found his body thrown in the road, and the donkey and the lion standing beside the body. The lion had not eaten the body or torn the donkey.

29 And the prophet took up the body of the man of Creator and laid it on the donkey and brought it back to the city to mourn and to bury him.

30 And he laid his body in his own grave. And they mourned over him, saying, Alas, my brother.

31 And after he had buried him, he said to his sons, When I die, bury me in the grave in which the man of Creator is buried — lay my bones beside his bones.

32 For the saying that he cried by the word of YHWH against the altar in Beit-El and against all the houses of the high places that are in the cities of Samaria shall surely come to pass.

33 After this thing Yarovam did not turn from his evil way, but made priests for the high places again from among all the people. Any who would, he ordained to be priests of the high places.

34 And this thing became sin to the house of Yarovam, so as to cut it off and to destroy it from the face of the earth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Aleph survives. The unnamed man of Creator from Yehudah delivers the covenant prophecy against the Beit-El altar — naming Yoshiyahu three hundred years before his birth — and then dies for disobeying the covenant word he himself delivered. The narrative explores the covenant principle that YHWH's word binds even those who deliver it.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. The lion that kills the man of Creator — verse 24 — but does not eat the body or harm the donkey — verse 28 — is read as the covenant sign that the judgment is from YHWH and is precise: punishing the disobedience without destroying more than YHWH decreed.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 2 — naming Yoshiyahu by name three centuries before his birth — is one of the most remarkable predictive covenant prophecies in the Nevi'im. Its fulfillment is recorded in Melakhim Bet 23:15-16, when Yoshiyahu burns human bones on the Beit-El altar.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18 — the old prophet said, I also am a prophet, and an angel spoke to me by the word of YHWH — but he lied to him. The covenant man of Creator is deceived not by an enemy but by a fellow prophet who lies about a divine word. The man of Creator accepts the false word, violates the genuine covenant command he received, and dies. The covenant warning is precise: the word of YHWH received directly binds unconditionally. No subsequent word — even from a prophet — can override it. The man of Creator should have known that YHWH's direct word to him could not be countermanded by another prophet's claim.

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 14 Akhiyah's Prophecy Against Yarovam — Yarovam's Son Dies — Rekhavam's Evil — Shishak Takes the Temple Treasures

1 At that time Aviyah the son of Yarovam fell sick.

2 And Yarovam said to his wife, Arise, and disguise yourself, that it not be known that you are the wife of Yarovam, and go to Shiloh. Behold, Akhiyah the prophet is there, who said of me that I should be king over this people.

3 Take with you ten loaves, some cakes, and a jar of honey, and go to him. He will tell you what shall happen to the child.

4 Yarovam's wife did so. She arose and went to Shiloh and came to the house of Akhiyah. Now Akhiyah could not see, for his eyes were dim because of his age.

5 And YHWH said to Akhiyah, Behold, the wife of Yarovam is coming to inquire of you concerning her son, for he is sick. Thus and thus shall you say to her, for when she comes, she will be disguising herself.

6 And when Akhiyah heard the sound of her feet as she came in at the door, he said, Come in, wife of Yarovam. Why do you pretend to be another? For I am charged with heavy news for you.

7 Go, tell Yarovam, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Because I exalted you from among the people and made you leader over my people Yisra'EL

8 And tore the kingdom away from the house of Dawid and gave it to you, and yet you have not been like my servant Dawid, who kept my commandments and followed me with all his heart, doing only that which was right in my eyes,

9 But you have done evil above all who were before you and have gone and made for yourself other gods and metal images, provoking me to anger, and have cast me behind your back —

10 Therefore behold, I will bring harm upon the house of Yarovam and will cut off from Yarovam every male, both bond and free in Yisra'EL, and will burn up the house of Yarovam as a man burns up dung until it is all gone.

11 Anyone belonging to Yarovam who dies in the city the dogs shall eat, and anyone who dies in the open country the birds of the heavens shall eat, for YHWH has spoken it.

12 Arise therefore, go to your house. When your feet enter the city, the child shall die.

13 And all Yisra'EL shall mourn for him and bury him, for he only of Yarovam shall come to the grave, because in him there is found something pleasing to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, in the house of Yarovam.

14 Moreover, YHWH will raise up for himself a king over Yisra'EL who shall cut off the house of Yarovam today. And henceforth,

15 YHWH will strike Yisra'EL as a reed is shaken in the water, and root up Yisra'EL out of this good land that he gave to their fathers and scatter them beyond the Euphrates, because they have made their Asherim, provoking YHWH to anger.

16 And he will give Yisra'EL up because of the sins of Yarovam, which he sinned and made Yisra'EL to sin.

17 Then Yarovam's wife arose and departed and came to Tirtzah. And as she came to the threshold of the house, the child died.

18 And all Yisra'EL buried him and mourned for him, according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke by his servant Akhiyah the prophet.

19 Now the rest of the acts of Yarovam, how he warred and how he reigned, behold, they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

20 And the time that Yarovam reigned was twenty-two years. And he slept with his fathers, and Nadav his son reigned in his place.

21 Now Rekhavam the son of Shelomoh reigned in Yehudah. Rekhavam was forty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Yerushalayim, the city that YHWH had chosen out of all the tribes of Yisra'EL, to put his name there. His mother's name was Na'amah the Ammonite.

22 And Yehudah did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, and they provoked him to jealousy with their sins that they committed, more than all that their fathers had done.

23 For they also built for themselves high places and pillars and Asherim on every high hill and under every green tree,

24 And there were also male cult prostitutes in the land. They did according to all the abominations of the nations that YHWH drove out before the people of Yisra'EL.

25 In the fifth year of King Rekhavam, Shishak king of Egypt came up against Yerushalayim.

26 He took away the treasures of the house of YHWH and the treasures of the king's house. He took away everything. He also took away all the shields of gold that Shelomoh had made.

27 And King Rekhavam made in their place shields of bronze, and committed them to the hands of the officers of the guard, who kept the door of the king's house.

28 And as often as the king went into the house of YHWH, the guard carried them and brought them back to the guardroom.

29 Now the rest of the acts of Rekhavam and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

30 And there was war between Rekhavam and Yarovam continually.

31 And Rekhavam slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of Dawid. His mother's name was Na'amah the Ammonite. And Aviyam his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Aleph survives. Akhiyah's prophecy against Yarovam — verses 7-16 — is the most comprehensive covenant indictment of the first northern king. The child who dies is the one person in Yarovam's house in whom YHWH found something pleasing — verse 13 — a deeply sobering covenant statement.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verse 15 — YHWH will strike Yisra'EL as a reed is shaken in the water, and root up Yisra'EL out of this good land and scatter them beyond the Euphrates — is the covenant prophecy of the Assyrian exile of the northern kingdom, spoken at the very beginning of the divided monarchy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 26 — Shishak king of Egypt took away the treasures of the house of YHWH and the treasures of the king's house — he took away everything. The golden shields of Shelomoh — taken. Replaced with bronze — verse 27. The covenant kingdom that began with gold ends its first generation with bronze. The progressive covenant impoverishment has begun.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — in him there is found something pleasing to YHWH, in the house of Yarovam. The dying child is the only member of Yarovam's house who has something pleasing to YHWH. He dies first, spared from the coming judgment. The covenant mercy embedded in the judgment: the one who is pleasing to YHWH is taken before the disaster. Verse 9 — you have cast me behind your back. The covenant accusation uses a physical image: Yarovam has turned his back on YHWH and walked away. The covenant relationship requires facing toward YHWH. To cast YHWH behind the back is to make him invisible — not because he has gone but because the covenant person has turned away.

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 15 Aviyam of Yehudah — Asa's Covenant Reform — Baasha Kills Nadav — War Between Yisra'EL and Yehudah

1 Now in the eighteenth year of King Yarovam the son of Nevat, Aviyam began to reign over Yehudah.

2 He reigned for three years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Ma'akhah the daughter of Avishalom.

3 And he walked in all the sins that his father did before him, and his heart was not wholly true to YHWH his Creator, as the heart of Dawid his father.

4 Nevertheless, for Dawid's sake YHWH his Creator gave him a lamp in Yerushalayim, setting up his son after him, and establishing Yerushalayim,

5 Because Dawid did what was right in the eyes of YHWH and did not turn aside from anything that he commanded him all the days of his life, except in the matter of Uriyah the Khittite.

6 Now there was war between Rekhavam and Yarovam all the days of his life.

7 The rest of the acts of Aviyam and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah? And there was war between Aviyam and Yarovam.

8 And Aviyam slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city of Dawid. And Asa his son reigned in his place.

9 In the twentieth year of Yarovam king of Yisra'EL, Asa began to reign over Yehudah,

10 And he reigned forty-one years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Ma'akhah the daughter of Avishalom.

11 And Asa did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, as Dawid his father had done.

12 He put away the male cult prostitutes out of the land and removed all the idols that his fathers had made.

13 He also removed Ma'akhah his mother from being queen mother because she had made an abominable image for Asherah. And Asa cut down her image and burned it at the brook Kidron.

14 But the high places were not taken away. Nevertheless, the heart of Asa was wholly true to YHWH all his days.

15 And he brought into the house of YHWH the sacred gifts of his father and his own sacred gifts — silver, gold, and vessels.

16 And there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Yisra'EL all their days.

17 Baasha king of Yisra'EL went up against Yehudah and built Ramah, that he might permit no one to go out or come in to Asa king of Yehudah.

18 Then Asa took all the silver and the gold that were left in the treasures of the house of YHWH and the treasures of the king's house and gave them into the hands of his servants. And King Asa sent them to Ben-Hadad the son of Tavrimmon, the son of Khezyon, king of Aram, who lived in Damascus, saying,

19 Let there be a covenant between me and you, as there was between my father and your father. Behold, I am sending to you a present of silver and gold. Go, break your covenant with Baasha king of Yisra'EL, that he may withdraw from me.

20 And Ben-Hadad listened to King Asa and sent the commanders of his armies against the cities of Yisra'EL and conquered Iyon, Dan, Avel Beit-Ma'akhah, and all Kinneroth, with all the land of Naftali.

21 And when Baasha heard of it, he stopped building Ramah and lived in Tirtzah.

22 Then King Asa made a proclamation to all Yehudah, none was exempt, and they carried away the stones of Ramah and its timber, with which Baasha had been building, and with them King Asa built Geva of Binyamin and Mitzpah.

23 Now the rest of all the acts of Asa, all his might, and all that he did, and the cities that he built, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah? But in his old age he was diseased in his feet.

24 And Asa slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of Dawid his father, and Yehoshafat his son reigned in his place.

25 Nadav the son of Yarovam began to reign over Yisra'EL in the second year of Asa king of Yehudah, and he reigned over Yisra'EL two years.

26 He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH and walked in the way of his father, and in his sin which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

27 Baasha the son of Akhiyah, of the house of Yissaskhar, conspired against him, and Baasha struck him down at Gibethon, which belonged to the Pelishtim, for Nadav and all Yisra'EL were laying siege to Gibethon.

28 So Baasha killed him in the third year of Asa king of Yehudah and reigned in his place.

29 And as soon as he was king, he killed all the house of Yarovam. He left to the house of Yarovam not one that breathed, until he had destroyed it, according to the word of YHWH that he spoke by his servant Akhiyah the Shilonite.

30 It was for the sins of Yarovam that he sinned and that he made Yisra'EL to sin, and because of the anger to which he provoked YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

31 Now the rest of the acts of Nadav and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

32 And there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Yisra'EL all their days.

33 In the third year of Asa king of Yehudah, Baasha the son of Akhiyah began to reign over all Yisra'EL at Tirtzah, and he reigned twenty-four years.

34 He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH and walked in the way of Yarovam and in his sin which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Aleph survives. Chapter 15 establishes the twin covenant evaluation formulas that will govern every king in Melakhim: for Yehudah's kings — did he do what was right in the eyes of YHWH as Dawid his father? For Yisra'EL's kings — did he walk in the sin of Yarovam which he made Yisra'EL to sin?

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Verse 5 — Dawid did what was right in the eyes of YHWH all the days of his life, except in the matter of Uriyah the Khittite — is the covenant benchmark against which every subsequent king is measured, with its single honest exception noted without minimizing it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 13 — Asa removed his own mother Ma'akhah from being queen mother because she had made an abominable image for Asherah. The covenant reform that required removing one's own mother from position of honor is the most personal expression of covenant priority in Asa's reign.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — for Dawid's sake YHWH his Creator gave him a lamp in Yerushalayim. The lamp of the Davidic covenant — promised in Chapter 11:36 — sustains the dynasty through kings who do not deserve it. Aviyam's heart was not wholly true to YHWH — verse 3 — yet the dynasty continues for Dawid's sake. The covenant made with Dawid in Shemu'el Bet 7 has more staying power than any individual king's faithfulness or failure. Verse 13 — Asa cut down his mother's Asherah image and burned it at the brook Kidron. The same brook where Dawid wept barefoot while fleeing Avshalom. Now Asa burns the covenant violation of his grandmother's family at the same location.

AKHAV AND ELIYAHU — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 18

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 16 Baasha Condemned — Zimri's Week as King — Omri Founds Samaria — Akhav Worse Than All Before Him

1 And the word of YHWH came to Yehu the son of Khanani against Baasha, saying,

2 Since I exalted you out of the dust and made you leader over my people Yisra'EL, and you have walked in the way of Yarovam and have made my people Yisra'EL to sin, provoking me to anger with their sins,

3 Behold, I will utterly sweep away Baasha and his house, and I will make your house like the house of Yarovam the son of Nevat.

4 Anyone belonging to Baasha who dies in the city the dogs shall eat, and anyone of his who dies in the field the birds of the heavens shall eat.

5 Now the rest of the acts of Baasha and what he did, and his might, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

6 And Baasha slept with his fathers and was buried at Tirtzah, and Elah his son reigned in his place.

7 Moreover, the word of YHWH came by the prophet Yehu the son of Khanani against Baasha and his house, both because of all the evil that he did in the sight of YHWH, provoking him to anger with the work of his hands, in being like the house of Yarovam, and also because he destroyed it.

8 In the twenty-sixth year of Asa king of Yehudah, Elah the son of Baasha began to reign over Yisra'EL at Tirtzah, and he reigned two years.

9 But his servant Zimri, commander of half his chariots, conspired against him. When he was at Tirtzah, drinking himself drunk in the house of Artzah, who was over the household at Tirtzah,

10 Zimri came in and struck him down and killed him, in the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Yehudah, and reigned in his place.

11 When he began to reign, as soon as he had seated himself on his throne, he struck down all the house of Baasha. He did not leave him a single male of his relatives or his friends.

12 Thus Zimri destroyed all the house of Baasha, according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke against Baasha by Yehu the prophet,

13 For all the sins of Baasha and the sins of Elah his son, which they sinned and which they made Yisra'EL to sin, provoking YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL to anger with their idols.

14 Now the rest of the acts of Elah and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

15 In the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Yehudah, Zimri reigned seven days in Tirtzah. Now the troops were encamped against Gibethon, which belonged to the Pelishtim.

16 And the troops who were encamped heard it said, Zimri has conspired, and he has also killed the king. Therefore all Yisra'EL made Omri, the commander of the army, king over Yisra'EL that day in the camp.

17 So Omri went up from Gibethon, and all Yisra'EL with him, and they besieged Tirtzah.

18 And when Zimri saw that the city was taken, he went into the citadel of the king's house and burned the king's house over him with fire and died,

19 Because of his sins that he committed, doing evil in the sight of YHWH, walking in the way of Yarovam, and for his sin which he did, making Yisra'EL to sin.

20 Now the rest of the acts of Zimri, and his conspiracy that he made, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

21 Then the people of Yisra'EL were divided into two parts. Half of the people followed Tibni the son of Ginat, to make him king, and half followed Omri.

22 But the people who followed Omri overcame the people who followed Tibni the son of Ginat. So Tibni died, and Omri reigned.

23 In the thirty-first year of Asa king of Yehudah, Omri began to reign over Yisra'EL, and he reigned for twelve years — six years he reigned in Tirtzah.

24 He bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer for two talents of silver, and he fortified the hill and called the name of the city that he built Samaria, after the name of Shemer, the owner of the hill.

25 Omri did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, and did more evil than all who were before him.

26 For he walked in all the way of Yarovam the son of Nevat and in the sins that he made Yisra'EL to sin, provoking YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, to anger by their idols.

27 Now the rest of the acts of Omri that he did, and the might that he showed, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

28 And Omri slept with his fathers and was buried in Samaria, and Akhav his son reigned in his place.

29 In the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Yehudah, Akhav the son of Omri began to reign over Yisra'EL, and Akhav the son of Omri reigned over Yisra'EL in Samaria twenty-two years.

30 And Akhav the son of Omri did evil in the sight of YHWH, more than all who were before him.

31 And as if it had been a light thing for him to walk in the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, he took for his wife Izevel the daughter of Etba'al, king of the Tzidonians, and went and served Ba'al and worshiped him.

32 He erected an altar for Ba'al in the house of Ba'al, which he built in Samaria.

33 And Akhav made an Asherah. Akhav did more to provoke YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, to anger than all the kings of Yisra'EL who were before him.

34 In his days Khiel of Beit-El built Yerikho. He laid its foundation at the cost of Aviram his firstborn, and set up its gates at the cost of his youngest son Seguv, according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke by Yehoshua the son of Nun.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Aleph survives. Chapter 16 chronicles the rapid succession of northern kings through assassination — Baasha, Elah, Zimri (seven days), the civil war between Omri and Tibni, and the establishment of the Omride dynasty. Each king is evaluated by the same standard: the sin of Yarovam which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verse 34 — Khiel of Beit-El built Yerikho at the cost of Aviram his firstborn and Seguv his youngest son — the fulfillment of Yehoshua's curse in Yehoshua 6:26. The covenant word spoken centuries earlier proves itself still operative in Akhav's day.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 31-33 — Akhav married Izevel — served Ba'al — worshiped him — erected an altar for Ba'al in Samaria — made an Asherah — did more than all the kings of Yisra'EL to provoke YHWH. The progressive covenant descent of the northern kingdom reaches its lowest point in Akhav.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31 — as if it had been a light thing for him to walk in the sins of Yarovam — he took Izevel and served Ba'al. The formula is devastating: the sins of Yarovam were the floor. Akhav treated them as a light thing — a starting point from which he descended further. The marriage to Izevel brought the worship of Ba'al from Tzidon into the covenant land at the state level. Verse 34 — the rebuilding of Yerikho fulfills Yehoshua's covenant curse five centuries old. The covenant word endures across all the political changes of dynasties and kingdoms. YHWH's curse spoken at Yerikho is still operative in Akhav's reign.

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 17 Eliyahu the Tishbite — The Drought — The Brook Kherit — The Widow of Tzarefat — The Dead Son Raised

1 Now Eliyahu the Tishbite, of Tishbe in Gil'ad, said to Akhav, As YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, lives, before whom I stand, there shall be neither dew nor rain these years, except by my word.

2 And the word of YHWH came to him:

3 Depart from here and turn eastward and hide yourself by the brook Kherit, which is east of the Yarden.

4 You shall drink from the brook, and I have commanded the ravens to feed you there.

5 So he went and did according to the word of YHWH. He went and lived by the brook Kherit that is east of the Yarden.

6 And the ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning, and bread and meat in the evening, and he drank from the brook.

7 And after a while the brook dried up, because there was no rain in the land.

8 Then the word of YHWH came to him,

9 Arise, go to Tzarefat, which belongs to Tzidon, and dwell there. Behold, I have commanded a widow there to feed you.

10 So he arose and went to Tzarefat. And when he came to the gate of the city, behold, a widow was there gathering sticks. And he called to her and said, Bring me a little water in a vessel, that I may drink.

11 And as she was going to bring it, he called to her and said, Bring me a morsel of bread in your hand.

12 And she said, As YHWH your Creator lives, I have nothing baked, only a handful of flour in a jar and a little oil in a jug. And now I am gathering a couple of sticks that I may go in and prepare it for myself and my son, that we may eat it and die.

13 And Eliyahu said to her, Do not fear — go and do as you have said. But first make me a little cake of it and bring it to me, and afterward make something for yourself and your son.

14 For thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, The jar of flour shall not be spent, and the jug of oil shall not be empty, until the day that YHWH sends rain upon the earth.

15 And she went and did as Eliyahu said. And she and he and her household ate for many days.

16 The jar of flour was not spent, neither did the jug of oil become empty, according to the word of YHWH that he spoke by Eliyahu.

17 After this the son of the woman, the mistress of the house, became ill. And his illness was so severe that there was no breath left in him.

18 And she said to Eliyahu, What have you against me, O man of Creator? You have come to me to bring my sin to remembrance and to cause the death of my son.

19 And he said to her, Give me your son. And he took him from her arms and carried him up into the upper chamber where he lodged, and laid him on his own bed.

20 And he cried to YHWH, O YHWH my Creator, have you brought calamity even upon the widow with whom I sojourn, by killing her son?

21 Then he stretched himself upon the child three times and cried to YHWH, O YHWH my Creator, let this child's soul come into him again.

22 And YHWH listened to the voice of Eliyahu. And the soul of the child came into him again, and he revived.

23 And Eliyahu took the child and brought him down from the upper chamber into the house and delivered him to his mother. And Eliyahu said, See, your son lives.

24 And the woman said to Eliyahu, Now I know that you are a man of Creator, and that the word of YHWH in your mouth is truth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Aleph survives. Chapter 17 introduces Eliyahu the Tishbite — one of the towering covenant figures of the entire Nevi'im. His appearance is abrupt and dramatic: without introduction, he stands before Akhav and declares the drought. The ravens feeding Eliyahu at the brook Kherit is the covenant provision that mirrors YHWH's feeding of Yisra'EL in the wilderness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. The widow of Tzarefat — verse 9-16 — is a Tzidonian woman, not an Israelite, who receives the covenant miracle of the inexhaustible flour and oil. YHWH sends the covenant prophet to a foreign widow in the homeland of Izevel, demonstrating that the covenant provision extends beyond the covenant community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 24 — the woman said, now I know that you are a man of Creator, and that the word of YHWH in your mouth is truth — is the covenant declaration that follows the resurrection of her son. The miracle of the flour and oil established Eliyahu's prophetic identity. The raising of the dead son establishes the truthfulness of YHWH's word in his mouth.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — as YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, lives, before whom I stand — there shall be neither dew nor rain these years, except by my word. Eliyahu's first recorded words are a covenant oath and a declaration of authority over the created order. He stands before YHWH — the posture of the covenant servant — and speaks with the authority of YHWH's word. The drought is the covenant response to Akhav's Ba'al worship: Ba'al was the storm god of Tzidon, the god of rain and fertility. YHWH withholds the very thing Ba'al was supposed to provide, demonstrating that Ba'al has no power over rain.

MELAKHIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 18 Eliyahu and the Prophets of Ba'al on Mount Karmel — The Fire of YHWH Falls — Ba'al Is Silent — The Rain Returns

1 After many days the word of YHWH came to Eliyahu, in the third year, saying, Go, show yourself to Akhav, and I will send rain on the earth.

2 So Eliyahu went to show himself to Akhav. Now the famine was severe in Samaria.

3 And Akhav called Ovadyah, who was over the household. Now Ovadyah feared YHWH greatly,

4 And when Izevel cut off the prophets of YHWH, Ovadyah took a hundred prophets and hid them by fifties in a cave and fed them with bread and water.

5 And Akhav said to Ovadyah, Go through the land to all the springs of water and to all the valleys. Perhaps we may find grass and save the horses and mules alive, and not lose some of the animals.

6 So they divided the land between them to pass through it. Akhav went in one direction by himself, and Ovadyah went in another direction by himself.

7 And as Ovadyah was on the way, behold, Eliyahu met him. And Ovadyah recognized him and fell on his face and said, Is it you, my lord Eliyahu?

8 And he answered him, It is I. Go, tell your lord, Behold, Eliyahu is here.

9 And he said, How have I sinned, that you would give your servant into the hand of Akhav, to kill me?

10 As YHWH your Creator lives, there is no nation or kingdom where my lord has not sent to seek you. And when they would say, He is not here, he would take an oath of the kingdom or nation, that they had not found you.

11 And now you say, Go, tell your lord, Eliyahu is here.

12 And as soon as I have gone from you, the Spirit of YHWH will carry you I know not where. And so, when I come and tell Akhav and he cannot find you, he will kill me, although I your servant have feared YHWH from my youth.

13 Has it not been told my lord what I did when Izevel killed the prophets of YHWH, how I hid a hundred men of YHWH's prophets by fifties in a cave and fed them with bread and water?

14 And now you say, Go, tell your lord, Eliyahu is here, and he will kill me.

15 And Eliyahu said, As YHWH of hosts lives, before whom I stand, I will surely show myself to him today.

16 So Ovadyah went to meet Akhav and told him. And Akhav went to meet Eliyahu.

17 When Akhav saw Eliyahu, Akhav said to him, Is it you, you troubler of Yisra'EL?

18 And he answered, I have not troubled Yisra'EL, but you have, and your father's house, because you have abandoned the commandments of YHWH and followed the Ba'als.

19 Now therefore send and gather all Yisra'EL to me at Mount Karmel, and the four hundred and fifty prophets of Ba'al and the four hundred prophets of Asherah, who eat at Izevel's table.

20 So Akhav sent to all the people of Yisra'EL and gathered the prophets together at Mount Karmel.

21 And Eliyahu came near to all the people and said, How long will you go limping between two different opinions? If YHWH is Creator, follow him. But if Ba'al, then follow him. And the people did not answer him a word.

22 Then Eliyahu said to the people, I, even I only, am left a prophet of YHWH, but Ba'al's prophets are four hundred and fifty men.

23 Let two bulls be given to us, and let them choose one bull for themselves and cut it in pieces and lay it on the wood, but put no fire to it. And I will prepare the other bull and lay it on the wood and put no fire to it.

24 And you call upon the name of your god, and I will call upon the name of YHWH, and the Creator who answers by fire, he is Creator. And all the people answered, It is well spoken.

25 Then Eliyahu said to the prophets of Ba'al, Choose for yourselves one bull and prepare it first, for you are many, and call upon the name of your god, but put no fire to it.

26 And they took the bull that was given them, and they prepared it and called upon the name of Ba'al from morning until noon, saying, O Ba'al, answer us. But there was no voice, and no one answered. And they limped around the altar that they had made.

27 And at noon Eliyahu mocked them, saying, Cry aloud, for he is a god. Either he is musing, or he is relieving himself, or he is on a journey, or perhaps he is asleep and must be awakened.

28 And they cried aloud and cut themselves after their custom with swords and lances, until the blood gushed out upon them.

29 And as midday passed, they raved on until the time of the offering of the oblation, but there was no voice. No one answered — no one paid attention.

30 Then Eliyahu said to all the people, Come near to me. And all the people came near to him. And he repaired the altar of YHWH that had been thrown down.

31 Eliyahu took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Yaakov, to whom the word of YHWH came, saying, Yisra'EL shall be your name.

32 And with the stones he built an altar in the name of YHWH. And he made a trench about the altar, as great as would contain two seahs of seed.

33 And he put the wood in order and cut the bull in pieces and laid it on the wood. And he said, Fill four jars with water and pour it on the burnt offering and on the wood.

34 And he said, Do it a second time. And they did it a second time. And he said, Do it a third time. And they did it a third time.

35 And the water ran around the altar and filled the trench also with water.

36 And at the time of the offering of the oblation, Eliyahu the prophet came near and said, O YHWH, Creator of Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yisra'EL, let it be known this day that you are Creator in Yisra'EL, and that I am your servant, and that I have done all these things at your word.

37 Answer me, O YHWH, answer me, that this people may know that you, O YHWH, are Creator, and that you have turned their hearts back.

38 Then the fire of YHWH fell and consumed the burnt offering and the wood and the stones and the dust, and licked up the water that was in the trench.

39 And when all the people saw it, they fell on their faces and said, YHWH, he is Creator — YHWH, he is Creator.

40 And Eliyahu said to them, Seize the prophets of Ba'al — let not one of them escape. And they seized them. And Eliyahu brought them down to the brook Kishon and slaughtered them there.

41 And Eliyahu said to Akhav, Go up, eat and drink, for there is a sound of the rushing of rain.

42 So Akhav went up to eat and to drink. And Eliyahu went up to the top of Mount Karmel. And he bowed himself down on the earth and put his face between his knees.

43 And he said to his servant, Go up now, look toward the sea. And he went up and looked and said, There is nothing. And he said, Go again, seven times.

44 And at the seventh time he said, Behold, a little cloud like a man's hand is rising from the sea. And he said, Go up, say to Akhav, Prepare your chariot and go down, lest the rain stop you.

45 And in a little while the heavens grew black with clouds and wind, and there was a great rain. And Akhav rode and went to Yizre'el.

46 And the hand of YHWH was on Eliyahu, and he gathered up his garment and ran before Akhav to the entrance of Yizre'el.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Aleph survives. The Mount Karmel contest — verses 20-40 — is the covenant confrontation between YHWH and Ba'al at its most dramatic. Eliyahu rebuilds the broken-down altar of YHWH with twelve stones representing the twelve tribes of the undivided covenant people — verse 31 — and calls for fire from heaven.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 36-37 — Eliyahu's prayer — O YHWH, Creator of Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yisra'EL, let it be known this day that you are Creator in Yisra'EL — answer me, that this people may know that you, O YHWH, are Creator — is read as the covenant prayer of covenant restoration: not Eliyahu's vindication but YHWH's covenant recognition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 39 — YHWH, he is Creator — YHWH, he is Creator — the people's twofold declaration after the fire falls is the covenant response that the entire contest was designed to produce. The question of verse 21 — if YHWH is Creator, follow him, but if Ba'al, follow him — is answered by fire.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21 — how long will you go limping between two different opinions? The Hebrew pisakhem — limping, hopping between two branches — is the image of a covenant people trying to maintain two covenant allegiances simultaneously. The contest at Karmel is designed to make the question undeniable: YHWH answers by fire; Ba'al does not answer at all. Verse 33-35 — Eliyahu drenches the altar and the trench with water — twelve jars total — making the covenant point unmistakable: the fire that falls will do so with every natural advantage removed. YHWH's covenant fire needs no human facilitation.

END OF PART THREE — CHAPTERS 13 THROUGH 18

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄
